title: web-tree-sitter must pair 0.24.x with tree-sitter-wasms and init once
summary: Kage's tree sitter extraction tier uses web tree sitter pure WASM, no node gyp with prebuilt grammars from tree sitter wasms. Two gotchas: 1 web tree sitter 0.25+/0.26 cannot load tree sitter wasms 0.1.x grammars built w
body:
Kage's tree-sitter extraction tier uses web-tree-sitter (pure WASM, no node-gyp) with prebuilt grammars from tree-sitter-wasms. Two gotchas: (1) web-tree-sitter 0.25+/0.26 cannot load tree-sitter-wasms 0.1.x grammars (built with tree-sitter-cli 0.20.8) — Language.load fails in getDylinkMetadata; the dependency must stay at web-tree-sitter ^0.24.7. (2) web-tree-sitter mutates its CommonJS export during Parser.init(), so a second require("web-tree-sitter") returns an object whose .init is not a function; the pre-init Parser class is cached once in kernel.ts (treeSitterRuntime) and reused for every Language.load. Per-language grammar npm packages (tree-sitter-python etc.) were rejected because they run node-gyp-build on install, which would break the npx install story.
Evidence: mcp/kernel.ts ensureTreeSitterLanguages; verified by loading python/go/rust/java/ruby grammars under web-tree-sitter 0.24.7 and reproducing both failure modes under 0.26.9 and double-require.
Verified by: npm test (190 pass) and acid index of pallets/click producing 1839 tree-sitter symbols
